review_summary: The transcript is sufficient for concept questions on the scaling bottlenecks of text-to-video diffusion and the purpose of diffusion distillation.

question_bank.question: What is the main scaling bottleneck in text-to-video diffusion compared with text-to-image diffusion?
choices: The token sequence becomes much longer because video adds a temporal dimension on top of spatial resolution
Video diffusion cannot use latent spaces, so it must always operate on raw pixels
Text-to-video models do not need a decoder, which shifts all cost to training only
The text encoder must be retrained from scratch for every prompt
question_bank.explanation: Video generation multiplies cost by adding many frames to already large spatial representations. This creates much longer token sequences and substantially higher computation.

question_bank.question: What is the goal of diffusion distillation?
choices: To reduce the number of denoising steps needed at inference while keeping quality as high as possible
To turn a diffusion model into a classifier that predicts labels directly
To force the model to use more denoising steps for better diversity
To replace the text encoder with a smaller CNN
question_bank.explanation: Standard diffusion sampling can require many model evaluations. Distillation tries to compress that iterative process into fewer steps, trading off speed and quality more favorably.
